enrichment.short_description: vLLM is an open-source, high-throughput inference and serving engine for large language models, licensed under Apache-2.0.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: vLLM: open-source, high-throughput LLM inference and serving engine with PagedAttention, broad hardware support, and an OpenAI-compatible API.

features.title: PagedAttention
features.description: Efficient memory management for attention keys/values that enables high-throughput serving of large language models.

features.title: Continuous batching
features.description: Dynamically batches incoming requests together to maximize GPU utilization and throughput.

features.title: OpenAI-compatible API server
features.description: Includes a server compatible with the OpenAI API format, plus an Anthropic Messages API, for drop-in integration.

features.title: Broad hardware support
features.description: Runs on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, Google TPUs, Intel Gaudi/XPU, AWS Neuron, Huawei Ascend NPU, Apple Silicon, and CPUs.

features.title: Quantization support
features.description: Supports FP8, INT8, INT4, GPTQ, AWQ, and GGUF quantization formats to reduce memory footprint.

features.title: Distributed inference
features.description: Supports tensor, pipeline, data, and expert parallelism for serving very large models across multiple devices.

features.title: Wide model compatibility
features.description: Supports 200+ Hugging Face model architectures, including decoder-only, mixture-of-experts, and multimodal models.

features.title: Multi-LoRA support
features.description: Serves multiple LoRA adapters simultaneously on top of a shared base model.

pros: Free and open source under the Apache-2.0 license
State-of-the-art serving throughput via PagedAttention and continuous batching
Supports a wide range of hardware backends beyond just NVIDIA GPUs
Large, active community with 2,000+ contributors and 86.5k+ GitHub stars
OpenAI-compatible API simplifies integration with existing tooling
cons: Requires self-hosting; there is no managed/hosted offering from the vLLM project itself
Requires GPU infrastructure and operational expertise to run at scale
No official commercial support or SLA from the project

faqs.question: Is vLLM free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. vLLM is fully open source under the Apache-2.0 license, with no paid tier offered by the project itself.

faqs.question: What license does vLLM use?
faqs.answer: vLLM is licensed under Apache-2.0.

faqs.question: What hardware does vLLM support?
faqs.answer: NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, Google TPUs, Intel Gaudi and XPU, AWS Neuron Accelerators, Huawei Ascend NPU, Apple Silicon, and CPU inference.

faqs.question: Does vLLM provide an OpenAI-compatible API?
faqs.answer: Yes, vLLM includes an OpenAI-compatible API server, along with support for tool calling and structured output.

faqs.question: Who maintains vLLM?
faqs.answer: vLLM originated at UC Berkeley's Sky Computing Lab and is now maintained by a community of over 2,000 contributors.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted LLM inference
use_cases.description: Teams deploy vLLM on their own GPU infrastructure to serve open-source LLMs with high throughput and low latency.

use_cases.title: Production API serving
use_cases.description: Organizations use vLLM's OpenAI-compatible API server to run self-hosted open-source models behind a familiar interface.

use_cases.title: Research and experimentation
use_cases.description: Researchers use vLLM to benchmark and serve new open-source model architectures efficiently.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is vLLM?
paragraphs: vLLM is an open-source library for large language model inference and serving, originally developed at UC Berkeley's Sky Computing Lab and now maintained by a community of more than 2,000 contributors. It is released under the Apache-2.0 license with no paid tier from the project itself.

The engine is built around PagedAttention and continuous batching to deliver high serving throughput, and it exposes an OpenAI-compatible API server so it can be dropped into existing tooling. It supports 200+ Hugging Face model architectures and runs across NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, Google TPUs, Intel Gaudi and XPU, AWS Neuron, Huawei Ascend NPU, Apple Silicon, and CPUs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who vLLM is for
paragraphs: vLLM suits engineering and research teams that want to self-host open-source LLMs with production-grade throughput, rather than relying solely on commercial hosted APIs. It requires infrastructure and GPU operations expertise since it is a self-hosted library, not a managed service.

enrichment.short_description: LiteLLM is an open-source LLM gateway providing a unified, OpenAI-compatible API to 100+ providers, with a paid Enterprise tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LiteLLM offers a unified OpenAI-compatible API for 100+ LLM providers, with an open-source core and a paid Enterprise tier for SSO and support.

features.title: Unified multi-provider API
features.description: A single OpenAI-compatible interface for calling 100+ LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and vLLM.

features.title: Spend tracking
features.description: Tracks and attributes LLM spend by API key, user, team, or organization.

features.title: Load balancing and fallbacks
features.description: Automatically retries and falls back across providers or models to improve reliability.

features.title: Virtual keys and budgets
features.description: Issues scoped virtual API keys with configurable budgets for teams and projects.

features.title: Guardrails and logging integrations
features.description: Integrates with observability tools such as Langfuse and Arize Phoenix, and supports guardrails.

features.title: Admin dashboard
features.description: Provides a dashboard for monitoring usage and managing access across an organization.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Usable as a Python SDK for direct integration or as a standalone proxy/gateway server.

pros: Open source with a genuinely free, full-featured self-hosted tier
Supports 100+ LLM providers through one OpenAI-compatible API
Built-in cost tracking, budgeting, and load balancing
Active project backed by Y Combinator (W23) with 52k+ GitHub stars
Used in production by companies such as Netflix and Lemonade, per the vendor's site
cons: Enterprise features (SSO, SCIM, 24/7 support, SLAs) require a paid, custom-priced plan
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Self-hosted deployment requires infrastructure management

faqs.question: Is LiteLLM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source version is free and includes core LLM integrations, spend tracking, logging, and guardrails. A separate Enterprise tier adds custom pricing.

faqs.question: How many LLM providers does LiteLLM support?
faqs.answer: LiteLLM supports 100+ providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Cohere, Mistral, and vLLM.

faqs.question: What's included in LiteLLM Enterprise?
faqs.answer: SSO, OIDC/JWT auth, SCIM, team/org admin controls, key rotation, secret manager integrations, 24/7 support, and SLAs.

faqs.question: Can I try LiteLLM Enterprise before buying?
faqs.answer: Yes, LiteLLM offers a 30-day trial key for the Enterprise tier.

faqs.question: Who builds LiteLLM?
faqs.answer: LiteLLM is built by BerriAI, a Y Combinator (W23) company.

use_cases.title: Multi-provider LLM routing
use_cases.description: Teams call 100+ LLM providers through a single OpenAI-compatible interface, avoiding vendor lock-in.

use_cases.title: Cost governance for LLM usage
use_cases.description: Organizations use LiteLLM's spend tracking and budgets to attribute and cap LLM costs per team or project.
use_cases.audience: Platform and finance teams overseeing AI spend

use_cases.title: Enterprise LLM gateway
use_cases.description: Larger organizations deploy LiteLLM Enterprise for SSO, audit logging, and SLA-backed support around an internal LLM gateway.

features: Key rotation and secret manager integration

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LiteLLM?
paragraphs: LiteLLM is an open-source LLM gateway built by BerriAI that gives developers a single, OpenAI-compatible API for calling more than 100 LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex AI. It can be used as a Python SDK or deployed as a standalone proxy/gateway server.

The open-source core is free and includes spend tracking, load balancing, fallbacks, virtual keys, and integrations with observability tools like Langfuse and Arize Phoenix. A separate LiteLLM Enterprise tier adds SSO, SCIM, team/org administration, key rotation, and 24/7 support with SLAs, priced on a custom, contact-sales basis.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LiteLLM is for
paragraphs: LiteLLM fits engineering teams that call multiple LLM providers and want a consistent API, centralized cost tracking, and reliability features like fallback routing, without building that layer themselves. Organizations needing SSO, audit logs, or contractual support can upgrade to the Enterprise tier.

enrichment.short_description: OpenRouter is a pay-as-you-go API platform providing unified access to 400+ AI models from 70+ providers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenRouter provides a single API for 400+ AI models across 70+ providers, with pay-as-you-go credits and some free-tier models.

features.title: Unified API
features.description: A single OpenAI SDK-compatible endpoint provides access to 400+ models across 70+ providers.

features.title: Automatic failover
features.description: Routes requests to alternate providers if one experiences downtime, improving availability.

features.title: Pay-as-you-go credits
features.description: No subscription required; users purchase credits that can be spent across any supported model.

features.title: Free models
features.description: A subset of models is usable at no cost, subject to daily request limits.

features.title: Bring your own key (BYOK)
features.description: Users can connect their own provider API keys; the first 1M requests per month are free, with a 5% fee afterward.

features.title: Provider and price routing
features.description: Requests can be routed by price or performance, with custom data policies for organizations.

pros: Access to 400+ models from 70+ providers through one API
No markup on inference costs beyond the direct provider price, per the vendor's site
Free models are available with daily rate limits, requiring no payment
Automatic provider fallback improves availability
Reports a large user base of 10M+ users, per the vendor's site
cons: No subscription/managed plan; usage is entirely pay-as-you-go via credits
Credit purchases via Stripe incur a 5.5% fee (minimum $0.80); crypto purchases incur 5%
Free models are rate-limited (50-1,000 requests/day) and unsuitable for production volume
Not open source

faqs.question: Is OpenRouter free to use?
faqs.answer: OpenRouter has no subscription fee; you pay for model usage via prepaid credits. A limited set of free models is available with daily rate limits.

faqs.question: How does OpenRouter pricing work?
faqs.answer: You purchase credits (5.5% fee via Stripe, minimum $0.80, or 5% via crypto) and spend them on inference at the underlying provider's list price.

faqs.question: What are OpenRouter's free model limits?
faqs.answer: 50 requests per day without purchased credits, or 1,000 requests per day once you've purchased at least $10 in credits.

faqs.question: Can I use my own API keys with OpenRouter?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYOK is supported. The first 1M requests per month are free, after which a 5% fee applies.

faqs.question: How many models does OpenRouter support?
faqs.answer: OpenRouter aggregates 400+ models from 70+ providers through a single API.

use_cases.title: Multi-model experimentation
use_cases.description: Developers test and compare many LLMs through one API without separate accounts for each provider.

use_cases.title: Provider redundancy
use_cases.description: Applications route requests through OpenRouter to automatically fail over to alternate providers during outages.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams building production AI apps

use_cases.title: Low-cost prototyping
use_cases.description: Builders use OpenRouter's free-tier models to prototype before committing to paid usage.

features: No markup over provider inference pricing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenRouter?
paragraphs: OpenRouter is a unified API platform that gives developers access to 400+ language models from 70+ providers through a single, OpenAI SDK-compatible endpoint. Rather than a subscription, it runs on prepaid credits: users buy credits (via Stripe, with a 5.5% fee and $0.80 minimum, or via crypto at 5%) and spend them across any supported model at the provider's list price.

The platform also offers a set of free models with daily rate limits (50 requests/day by default, rising to 1,000/day after purchasing $10 or more in credits), automatic failover between providers, and support for bringing your own provider API keys.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenRouter is for
paragraphs: OpenRouter suits developers and teams who want to try or switch between many LLMs without managing separate provider accounts, and applications that need automatic provider fallback for reliability. Its free models and low entry cost also make it accessible for prototyping and hobby projects.

enrichment.short_description: Together AI is a cloud platform offering serverless inference, fine-tuning, and GPU clusters for open-source AI models.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Together AI offers pay-per-token inference, fine-tuning, and on-demand GPU clusters (H100/H200/B200) for open-source AI models.

features.title: Serverless inference
features.description: On-demand, pay-per-token access to chat, vision, image, video, audio, and embedding models.

features.title: Fine-tuning
features.description: Supports SFT and DPO fine-tuning of open-source models, priced per 1M tokens and scaling with model size.

features.title: Dedicated and provisioned inference
features.description: Reserved throughput and dedicated model/container inference with up to 99% uptime SLA.

features.title: GPU clusters
features.description: On-demand or reserved (181+ day) access to H100, H200, and B200 GPUs for training and compute.

features.title: Batch inference
features.description: Asynchronous processing for large-scale inference workloads.

features.title: Managed storage and sandboxes
features.description: Storage optimized for AI workloads plus code sandbox environments, each billed separately.

pros: Wide range of serverless models with transparent per-token pricing
Combines fine-tuning, dedicated inference, and raw GPU cluster access on one platform
Reserved GPU pricing (181+ days) is cheaper than on-demand rates
Backed by significant funding, including an $800M Series C, per the vendor's site
Purchased credits never expire, per Together AI's billing documentation
cons: No free trial; Together AI's documentation states a minimum $5 credit purchase is required to access the platform
Reserved/dedicated capacity pricing for some tiers requires contacting sales
Fully prepaid model — API access stops if the account balance reaches zero

faqs.question: Does Together AI offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No. Together AI's documentation states it does not currently offer free trials.

faqs.question: What's the minimum to start using Together AI?
faqs.answer: A minimum $5 credit purchase is required to access the platform.

faqs.question: What GPUs does Together AI offer?
faqs.answer: On-demand and reserved access to NVIDIA H100 ($3.99/GPU-hour on-demand), H200 ($5.99/GPU-hour), and B200 ($8.19/GPU-hour).

faqs.question: Does Together AI support fine-tuning?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports SFT and DPO fine-tuning priced per 1M tokens, with pricing that scales with model size.

faqs.question: When was Together AI founded?
faqs.answer: Together AI was founded in 2022.

use_cases.title: Serverless model inference
use_cases.description: Developers call open-source and proprietary models on demand via a token-priced API without managing infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Model fine-tuning
use_cases.description: Teams fine-tune open-source models using SFT or DPO on Together AI's managed infrastructure.

use_cases.title: GPU cluster training
use_cases.description: Organizations rent on-demand or reserved H100/H200/B200 GPU clusters for large-scale training or inference.

use_cases.title: Production-grade dedicated inference
use_cases.description: Companies use provisioned throughput and dedicated inference with uptime SLAs for production workloads.

pricing_plans.price: From $0.03 per 1M input tokens (varies by model)
features: Chat, vision, image, video, audio, and embedding models
$5 minimum credit purchase to access the platform

pricing_plans.price: From $0.48 per 1M tokens (SFT, models up to 16B)
features: Pricing scales with model size (16B to 100B+)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Together AI?
paragraphs: Together AI is a cloud platform, founded in 2022, for running, fine-tuning, and training AI models. It offers serverless inference priced per token, fine-tuning (SFT and DPO) for open-source models, dedicated and provisioned inference with SLAs, and on-demand or reserved GPU clusters (H100, H200, B200).

The platform is fully prepaid: Together AI's documentation states it does not currently offer free trials and requires a minimum $5 credit purchase to access the platform, though purchased credits never expire.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Together AI is for
paragraphs: Together AI fits teams that want to run or fine-tune open-source models without managing their own GPU infrastructure, as well as organizations that need raw, on-demand or reserved GPU capacity for training and large-scale inference.

enrichment.short_description: Fireworks AI is a pay-as-you-go inference platform for running, fine-tuning, and deploying open-source generative AI models at scale.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fireworks AI offers pay-per-token inference, fine-tuning, and dedicated GPU deployments for open-source LLM, vision, and audio models.

features.title: Serverless inference
features.description: Pay-per-token access to 30+ hosted open-source models across LLM, vision, image, and audio categories.

features.title: On-demand GPU deployments
features.description: Dedicated deployments on H100, H200, B200, and B300 GPUs, billed hourly from $7.00/hour, with multi-region and custom model support.

features.title: Reserved capacity
features.description: Guaranteed capacity and higher quotas for predictable, high-volume workloads.

features.title: Fine-tuning and LoRA
features.description: Supervised and preference fine-tuning (SFT, DPO), priced per million training tokens, with LoRA and full-parameter options.

features.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning
features.description: RL-based fine-tuning billed per GPU hour at on-demand deployment rates.

features.title: OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs
features.description: Drop-in API compatibility to ease migration from other model providers.

features.title: Cached and batch token discounts
features.description: Cached input tokens and batch inference are billed at 50% of standard rates.

pros: Pay-per-token serverless pricing with no infrastructure to manage
Wide model catalog spanning LLM, vision, image, and audio
OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs simplify migration
50% discounts on cached and batch inference tokens
Dedicated on-demand GPU deployments for high-throughput workloads
cons: No ongoing free plan, only an initial $1 credit for new accounts
Usage-based billing across many models and GPU tiers makes cost estimation complex
Reserved and enterprise pricing require contacting sales
Requires API/code integration; no no-code interface

faqs.question: Does Fireworks AI offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: New accounts receive $1 in free credits to test the serverless API before paying.

faqs.question: What pricing model does Fireworks AI use?
faqs.answer: Pay-per-token serverless pricing, plus on-demand and reserved GPU deployments billed hourly, starting at $7.00/hour for an H100 80GB.

faqs.question: Can I fine-tune models on Fireworks AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports LoRA and full-parameter supervised (SFT) and preference (DPO) fine-tuning, priced per million training tokens.

faqs.question: Is Fireworks AI compatible with OpenAI's API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fireworks provides OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible API endpoints.

faqs.question: What GPUs are available for on-demand deployments?
faqs.answer: H100 80GB, H200 141GB, B200 180GB, and B300 288GB, billed hourly from $7.00 up to $12.00.

use_cases.title: Serving open-source LLMs in production
use_cases.description: Teams deploy chat, code, and reasoning models like DeepSeek, GLM, and Qwen behind an OpenAI-compatible API without managing GPU infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Fine-tuning models on proprietary data
use_cases.description: ML engineers run LoRA or full-parameter SFT/DPO fine-tuning jobs priced per million training tokens.

use_cases.title: High-throughput dedicated inference
use_cases.description: Companies with steady traffic reserve on-demand GPU capacity (H100/H200/B200/B300) instead of paying per token.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fireworks AI?
paragraphs: Fireworks AI is an inference platform for running, fine-tuning, and serving open-source generative AI models, including LLM, vision, image, and audio models such as DeepSeek, GLM, Kimi, Qwen, Gemma, and FLUX.
Pricing is pay-per-token on the serverless tier, with on-demand dedicated GPU deployments (H100, H200, B200, B300) and reserved capacity for high-volume or latency-sensitive workloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fireworks AI is for
paragraphs: Fireworks AI suits engineering and ML teams that want to serve or fine-tune open-source models via an OpenAI-compatible API without managing their own GPU infrastructure, from early-stage prototypes on the serverless tier to scale-stage companies needing reserved or dedicated capacity.

enrichment.short_description: Replicate lets developers run, fine-tune, and deploy open-source machine learning models through a pay-per-second API.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Replicate is a pay-per-use API for running thousands of open-source ML models and deploying custom models with Cog.

features.title: Run community-published models
features.description: Access thousands of open-source models covering image, video, speech, and language tasks with a few lines of code.

features.title: Fine-tune image models
features.description: Customize image models such as SDXL with your own training data.

features.title: Custom model deployment with Cog
features.description: Cog is Replicate's open-source packaging tool for deploying proprietary models to Replicate's infrastructure.

features.title: Autoscaling infrastructure
features.description: Deployments scale up and down automatically, including to zero, with no charges while idle.

features.title: Built-in monitoring
features.description: Logging and performance metrics are included for deployed models.

features.title: Hardware-based billing
features.description: Per-second pricing across CPU and GPU types, from small CPU instances up to 8x A100 80GB.

pros: Pay-per-second billing with no charges when scaled to zero
Large catalog of community and major-lab models, including from OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic
Cog packaging tool is open source, easing custom model deployment
Enterprise plans include dedicated account managers and volume discounts
cons: No published free tier or trial credits
Costs can be hard to predict across many differently priced hardware and model combinations
Requires code/API integration; no no-code interface
Now part of Cloudflare following its 2025 acquisition, which could shift its long-term roadmap

faqs.question: Does Replicate offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No published free tier or trial credits were found; billing is pay-per-second or pay-per-output from the first request.

faqs.question: What is Cog?
faqs.answer: Cog is Replicate's open-source tool for packaging machine learning models so they can be deployed on Replicate's infrastructure.

faqs.question: Is Replicate still an independent product?
faqs.answer: Yes. Following its acquisition by Cloudflare (announced November 2025), Replicate continues operating under its own brand with the same API.

faqs.question: How is Replicate priced?
faqs.answer: Most models are billed per second of hardware usage (CPU or GPU); some hosted models are billed per token, image, or video second instead.

faqs.question: What hardware is available for private model deployments?
faqs.answer: CPU and GPU options including Nvidia T4, L40S, and A100 80GB, with multi-GPU configurations up to 8x.

use_cases.title: Running pre-built AI models via API
use_cases.description: Developers call thousands of open-source models (image, video, speech, LLM) with a few lines of code without hosting infrastructure themselves.

use_cases.title: Fine-tuning image generation models
use_cases.description: Teams fine-tune models like SDXL on their own images for branded or custom outputs.

use_cases.title: Deploying proprietary models at scale
use_cases.description: Organizations package custom models with Cog and deploy them behind an autoscaling API.

features: No charge when scaled to zero
GPU options from T4 to 8x A100 80GB

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Replicate?
paragraphs: Replicate is a cloud API platform for running open-source machine learning models, including image, video, speech, and language models from organizations like OpenAI, Google, and Black Forest Labs.
Developers can also package and deploy their own models using Cog, Replicate's open-source model-packaging tool, with infrastructure that autoscales to zero when idle.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Replicate is for
paragraphs: Replicate suits developers who want to call open-source AI models via API without hosting GPUs themselves, as well as ML teams deploying proprietary models on autoscaling infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Modal is a serverless cloud platform for running Python code, AI inference, and training workloads with usage-based GPU pricing.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Starter plan includes $30/month in compute credits)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Modal is a serverless Python cloud for AI inference and training, with a free tier of $30/month in compute credits.

features.title: Python-native SDK
features.description: Deploy code to the cloud directly from Python without separate infrastructure configuration.

features.title: Sub-second cold starts
features.description: Instant autoscaling across GPUs with sub-second cold-start times.

features.title: Multi-GPU support
features.description: Jobs scale up to 128 B200 GPUs with high-speed networking.

features.title: Global GPU infrastructure
features.description: Compute runs across multiple clouds and regions.

features.title: Production observability
features.description: Integrated logging and monitoring for deployed functions.

features.title: Broad workload support
features.description: Runs LLM inference, multi-modal inference, fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and coding agent workloads.

features.title: Granular usage-based billing
features.description: Per-second billing for GPU, CPU, and memory, e.g. Nvidia H100 at $0.001097/second.

pros: Genuine free tier: $30/month in compute credits on the Starter plan
Per-second billing for GPU, CPU, and memory keeps costs granular
Sub-second cold starts for GPU-backed functions
Python-native workflow with no separate infrastructure config
Academic grants up to $10,000 for researchers and graduate students
cons: Team plan adds a $250/month base fee on top of compute usage
Enterprise features like HIPAA compliance and SSO require contacting sales
Starter plan is capped at 3 seats and 100 containers
Usage-based GPU/CPU/memory pricing requires active cost monitoring

faqs.question: Does Modal have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free and includes $30/month in compute credits, up to 3 seats, and 100 containers.

faqs.question: How is Modal priced?
faqs.answer: Per-second billing for GPU, CPU, and memory (e.g., Nvidia H100 at $0.001097/second), plus a $250/month base fee on the Team plan.

faqs.question: What GPUs does Modal support?
faqs.answer: GPUs including Nvidia B300, H100, A100 80GB, L4, and T4, with multi-GPU jobs scaling up to 128 B200s.

faqs.question: Does Modal offer academic pricing?
faqs.answer: Yes, academic grants of up to $10,000 are available for graduate students and researchers.

faqs.question: What compliance features does Modal offer?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan includes HIPAA compliance, Okta SSO, and audit logs.

use_cases.title: Serving AI inference in production
use_cases.description: Teams deploy LLM and multi-modal inference endpoints with sub-second cold starts on autoscaling GPUs.

use_cases.title: Running Python batch and training jobs
use_cases.description: Data scientists run large-scale batch processing, fine-tuning, and RL rollouts using Modal's Python SDK without managing servers.

use_cases.title: Academic and research compute
use_cases.description: Graduate students and researchers access GPU compute through Modal's academic grant program.

features: Custom domains, static IP proxy, deployment rollbacks
Unlimited seats and higher GPU concurrency
Audit logs, Okta SSO, HIPAA compliance

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Modal?
paragraphs: Modal is a serverless cloud compute platform for running Python code, AI inference, training, batch processing, and sandboxes, with sub-second cold starts and instant autoscaling across GPUs.
The Starter plan is free and includes $30/month in compute credits; paid plans add per-second GPU, CPU, and memory billing, with the Team plan starting at $250/month plus usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Modal is for
paragraphs: Modal suits Python developers and ML teams that need on-demand GPU or CPU compute for inference, training, or batch jobs, from individuals using the free Starter tier to enterprises needing HIPAA compliance and SSO.

enrichment.short_description: RunPod is a cloud GPU platform for training, fine-tuning, and deploying AI models with pay-per-second, no-contract pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RunPod offers on-demand and serverless GPU cloud compute for AI training and inference, billed per second with no contracts.

features.description: Reserved or Spot GPU instances for development and long-running jobs, billed hourly or per second.

features.title: Serverless autoscaling

features.title: Instant Clusters
features.description: Multi-GPU distributed compute for large-scale training.

features.title: Wide GPU selection
features.description: 30+ GPU SKUs, from RTX 3090 to B300, available across 31 global regions.

features.title: Persistent network storage
features.description: Network storage with no egress fees.

features.title: Zero idle cost
features.description: Serverless endpoints incur no charges while inactive.

features.title: Compliance and reliability
features.description: SOC 2 Type II compliance, network isolation options, and 99.9%-99.99% uptime SLA.

pros: Per-second billing with no contracts or minimum commitments
Wide GPU selection (30+ SKUs) from RTX 3090 to B300
Serverless endpoints cost nothing when idle
No egress fees on persistent network storage
SOC 2 Type II compliance available
cons: No published free tier or trial credits
Spot instances are interruptible, unsuitable for uninterrupted workloads
Pricing varies widely across 30+ GPU SKUs, complicating cost planning
Reserved capacity discounts require contacting sales for enterprise-scale deployments

faqs.question: Does RunPod offer a free tier?
faqs.answer: No free tier or trial credits are published on RunPod's pricing page.

faqs.question: How is RunPod priced?
faqs.answer: Pods are billed hourly or per second by GPU type, from $0.27/hour for an RTX A5000 up to $7.39/hour for a B300, and Serverless is billed based on actual usage.

faqs.question: What is FlashBoot?
faqs.answer: FlashBoot is RunPod's technology for sub-200ms cold starts on Serverless endpoints.

faqs.question: Does RunPod charge for idle Serverless endpoints?
faqs.answer: No, Serverless endpoints cost nothing when idle and autoscale from zero.

faqs.question: When was RunPod founded?
faqs.answer: RunPod was founded on October 31, 2022.

use_cases.title: Training and fine-tuning models on dedicated GPUs
use_cases.description: ML engineers rent Reserved or Spot GPU Pods by the hour to train or fine-tune models without buying hardware.

use_cases.title: Serving AI inference at scale
use_cases.description: Developers deploy Serverless endpoints that autoscale to zero and back, paying only for active usage.

use_cases.title: Multi-GPU distributed training
use_cases.description: Organizations use Instant Clusters for multi-node, multi-GPU training jobs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RunPod?
paragraphs: RunPod is a cloud GPU platform offering on-demand Pods, autoscaling Serverless endpoints, and Instant Clusters for AI training, fine-tuning, and inference.
Pricing is per-second or hourly across 30+ GPU SKUs, from $0.27/hour for an RTX A5000 up to $7.39/hour for a B300, with no contracts or minimum commitments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RunPod is for
paragraphs: RunPod suits ML engineers and AI teams that need on-demand GPU capacity for training or inference without long-term contracts, from single-GPU experimentation to multi-node distributed training via Instant Clusters.

enrichment.short_description: Baseten is a managed inference platform for deploying and scaling AI/ML models in production, with cloud, self-hosted, and hybrid deployment options.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore Baseten, an AI model inference platform offering dedicated deployments, optimized model APIs, and self-hosted or hybrid production infrastructure.

features.title: Dedicated Inference
features.description: Deploy custom, open-source, and fine-tuned models at scale on dedicated compute.

features.title: Pre-optimized Model APIs
features.description: Call pre-optimized foundation and open-source models (LLMs, embeddings, transcription, image generation) with per-token pricing.

features.title: Model Training
features.description: Train models and deploy them from the same platform using on-demand compute.

features.title: Baseten Chains
features.description: Orchestrate compound AI pipelines with granular hardware control for each step of the workflow.

features.title: Global Infrastructure
features.description: Cross-cloud deployment infrastructure with fast cold starts and a claimed 99.99% uptime.

features.title: Self-hosted and hybrid deployment
features.description: Run models inside a customer's own VPC, or combine self-hosted capacity with Baseten Cloud.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: Platform infrastructure is SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant.

pros: Supports fully managed cloud, self-hosted, and hybrid deployment models
Usage-based pricing on the Basic tier avoids a fixed monthly minimum
SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance built into the platform
Published customer list includes Notion, HubSpot, Cursor, ClickUp, and Harvey
Free credits available for new accounts to explore the platform
cons: Pro and Enterprise plan pricing is quote-only and not published
Priority GPU access, custom SLAs, and self-host deployments require contacting sales
Usage-based compute costs (per-token, per-minute) can be harder to predict than flat-rate plans

faqs.question: Does Baseten offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Baseten's Basic tier is $0/month with pay-as-you-go usage billing, and new accounts receive free credits to explore the platform.

faqs.question: Can Baseten be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes. Baseten supports self-hosted deployment in a customer's own VPC, as well as hybrid setups combining self-hosted capacity with Baseten Cloud.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Baseten have?
faqs.answer: Baseten states its platform is SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant.

faqs.question: How is Baseten priced?
faqs.answer: Baseten uses usage-based pricing: per-token rates for Model APIs and per-minute/hour billing for dedicated GPU/CPU deployments. Pro and Enterprise tiers add volume discounts available on request.

use_cases.title: Deploying custom and fine-tuned models
use_cases.description: Teams deploy custom, open-source, or fine-tuned ML models to dedicated infrastructure with autoscaling.
use_cases.audience: ML engineers and AI product teams

use_cases.title: Serving pre-optimized foundation models
use_cases.description: Developers call pre-optimized model APIs (LLMs, embeddings, transcription, image generation) with per-token pricing instead of managing their own inference infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Compound AI pipelines
use_cases.description: Baseten Chains lets teams orchestrate multi-step AI pipelines with independent hardware control for each step.
use_cases.audience: AI infrastructure teams building multi-model workflows

use_cases.title: Regulated or high-security workloads
use_cases.description: SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance, plus self-hosted/hybrid deployment options, support teams with strict data residency or compliance requirements.
use_cases.audience: Enterprises in healthcare, finance, or other regulated industries

features: SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Baseten?
paragraphs: Baseten is an inference platform for deploying and scaling AI and ML models in production. It offers dedicated deployments for custom, open-source, and fine-tuned models, along with pre-optimized model APIs for common workloads like LLMs, embeddings, transcription, and image generation.

The platform can be used as a fully managed cloud service (Baseten Cloud), self-hosted inside a customer's own VPC, or in a hybrid configuration that combines both. Its infrastructure is SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Baseten is for
paragraphs: Baseten is aimed at ML engineers and AI product teams that need to move models from experimentation to production without building and maintaining their own inference infrastructure, as well as regulated organizations that need self-hosted or hybrid deployment for data residency reasons.

enrichment.short_description: Anyscale is a managed AI platform built on the open-source Ray framework for scaling distributed Python, ML training, and LLM workloads.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Anyscale offers a managed, pay-as-you-go platform built on Ray for scaling AI training, batch inference, and distributed Python workloads.

features.title: Managed Ray platform
features.description: Fully managed infrastructure for Ray, the open-source distributed computing framework originally built at UC Berkeley's RISELab.

features.title: Distributed model training
features.description: Scale model training jobs across pooled GPU clusters using simple Python decorator-based APIs.

features.title: Multimodal data processing
features.description: Curate and process large-scale video, image, text, and audio datasets.

features.title: Batch embeddings and inference
features.description: Generate batch embeddings and run large-scale batch inference workloads.

features.title: Multi-cloud orchestration
features.description: Deploy across AWS, GCP, Azure, Nebius, and CoreWeave.

features.title: Fine-grained hardware allocation
features.description: Allocate CPU, GPU, and TPU resources at the workload level.

features.title: Enterprise access controls
features.description: SSO, SAML, SCIM, and audit logs are available for BYOC and enterprise deployments.

features.title: Native framework support
features.description: Built-in support for PyTorch, vLLM, and XGBoost through Ray libraries.

pros: Built and maintained by the original creators of the open-source Ray framework
Offers both fully hosted and Bring-Your-Own-Cloud (BYOC) deployment models
Multi-cloud support across AWS, GCP, Azure, Nebius, and CoreWeave
$100 in free credits available for new users to try the platform
Enterprise-grade access controls (SSO, SAML, SCIM, audit logs) on BYOC/enterprise tiers
cons: Pricing for higher-end GPU families (H/B/GB series) is quote-only
Hosted tier is limited to VM deployments in a limited set of regions
Getting full value requires familiarity with Ray's distributed Python programming model

faqs.question: What is Anyscale built on?
faqs.answer: Anyscale is a managed platform built on Ray, the open-source distributed computing framework originally created at UC Berkeley's RISELab.

faqs.question: Does Anyscale have a free tier?
faqs.answer: New users receive $100 in Anyscale credits at signup, plus low-cost template projects, though there is no permanent free plan.

faqs.question: Can Anyscale run in my own cloud?
faqs.answer: Yes. Anyscale offers a Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC) option that runs on any cloud, region, or on-premises environment inside the customer's own VPC.

faqs.question: What clouds does Anyscale support?
faqs.answer: Anyscale supports multi-cloud orchestration across AWS, GCP, Azure, Nebius, and CoreWeave.

faqs.question: How is Anyscale priced?
faqs.answer: Anyscale uses pay-as-you-go compute pricing, from $0.0135/hr for CPU-only instances up to about $4.96/hr for NVIDIA A100 GPUs, with contact-for-pricing on higher-end GPU families and committed-use contracts for volume discounts.

use_cases.title: Distributed model training
use_cases.description: Teams scale model training jobs across pooled GPU clusters using Ray's Python APIs without hand-managing cluster infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Batch inference and embeddings
use_cases.description: Organizations generate embeddings and run large-scale batch inference jobs across multimodal data (video, image, text, audio).

use_cases.title: Post-training LLM workloads
use_cases.description: Teams run post-training workloads using frameworks like SkyRL and veRL on Anyscale's managed Ray infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Enterprise BYOC deployments
use_cases.description: Enterprises with data residency or compliance needs run Anyscale inside their own cloud VPC or on-premises with SSO/SAML/SCIM access controls.

features: Deploy on any cloud, region, or on-premises
Runs inside the customer's own VPC
24/7 enterprise support with unlimited case submissions

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Anyscale?
paragraphs: Anyscale is a managed AI infrastructure platform built around Ray, the open-source distributed computing framework. It lets teams scale distributed model training, multimodal data processing, batch embeddings, and post-training LLM workloads across pooled GPU clusters using simple Python APIs.

Anyscale can run as a hosted service on Anyscale-managed infrastructure or as Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC), deploying into a customer's own VPC across AWS, GCP, Azure, Nebius, or CoreWeave, with enterprise access controls such as SSO, SAML, SCIM, and audit logs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Anyscale is for
paragraphs: Anyscale is aimed at ML engineers and AI platform teams already using or considering Ray for distributed Python workloads, as well as enterprises that need BYOC deployment with strong access controls for data residency or compliance reasons.
